purpose: Assesses EM reserve adequacy by measuring whether reserves cover short-term external debt, with ratio below 1.0 indicating vulnerability.
components: Ratio = reserves / short-term external debt (maturity < 1 year)
Below 1.0 indicates inability to cover short-term obligations without market access
Currently 12-15 EM economies below 1.0 vs. 8-10 in 2019
Bifurcation: commodity exporters at 15-25 months import cover, importers at 2-4 months
Country examples: Saudi $420B (18+ months) vs. Pakistan $9B (~2 months)
current_assessment: Reserve coverage has deteriorated with 12-15 economies below the critical 1.0 threshold. Sharp bifurcation between commodity exporters and importers. Country-level granularity shows extreme vulnerability concentration in import-dependent managed-rate economies (Egypt, Pakistan, Ethiopia).
limitations: Reserves can be window-dressed through swaps and other off-balance-sheet arrangements
Does not capture corporate dollar debt vulnerability (original sin reloaded)
Import cover months assume constant import levels; crisis typically compresses imports
Effective liquid reserves may differ significantly from reported reserves (China: ~$2.0-2.2T liquid vs. $3.2T reported)
